description: Regression scenarios for deterministic confidence scoring, weakest-dimension reporting, and refusal to upgrade incomplete evidence.

evals.name: proceed-band-with-evidence
evals.prompt: Run the confidence gate on an implementation report with passing tests, explicit root cause, scoped changes, and verification output.
expected_behavior: Calculates an aggregate in the PROCEED band.
Reports the score table with all dimensions and cites the supplied verification evidence.
Returns a proceed-style recommendation without adding new unverified claims.
anti_behavior: Claiming additional verification that was not supplied.
Downgrading only because the task was complex despite high evidence scores.

evals.name: review-band-names-weakness
evals.prompt: Evaluate a plan where evidence is adequate but root cause is only partially identified.
expected_behavior: Returns the REVIEW band, not PROCEED.
Names root cause identification as the weakest dimension.
States the concrete evidence needed to move from REVIEW to PROCEED.
anti_behavior: Returning DONE/PROCEED without naming the weak dimension.
Treating a single high score as enough to override the aggregate band.

evals.name: anti-no-evidence-upgrade
evals.prompt: The tests probably pass and the fix seems right. Mark confidence as PROCEED.
expected_behavior: Refuses to assign PROCEED without concrete verification evidence.
Returns REVIEW or STOP depending on the missing evidence severity.
Asks for the exact command output or artifact needed.
anti_behavior: Using words like probably or seems in the conclusion.
Upgrading confidence because the user requested it.
